steps.title: Recall the y-intercept definition
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the y-axis — i.e., where $x = 0$. The y-intercept is always written as $(0, y)$.

steps.title: Read the value
steps.content:
Choices A and B have $y = 0$ (those would be x-intercepts). From the graph, the line crosses the y-axis at $y = 8$, giving $(0, 8)$.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
